comment: Cross-language behavioral contract for the session-events artifact (`events/<name>.ndjson`).
This file is the single source of truth for the two rules both implementations must agree on:
(1) fileNames — how a file name maps to a stream name (including the legacy `.json` style-segment
strip), implemented by Kotlin `SessionEvents.parseFileName` and TS `parseStreamFileName`;
(2) lines — how one NDJSON line decodes to an ordering timestamp + payload (the `{timeMs, data}`
envelope rule), implemented by the Kotlin `SessionEventsReader` and TS `decodeEventLine`.
In `lines`, `payload: null` means the line is skipped entirely; `t: null` means the line carries
no ordering timestamp (the JVM reader represents that as 0). To change either rule: update both
implementations AND this fixture in the same change — never one language's tests alone.
